steps.title: Block the shell (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a wide flattened oval for the shell — clearly wider than tall. This is the body of the crab seen from above.

steps.title: Plan the claws (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch two small ovals out in front of the shell on either side for the claws. Connect each one to the shell with a short straight guide line — those will become the arms.

steps.title: Mark the legs (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch three short angled guide lines on each side of the shell for the legs — angling out and slightly back. The legs get a touch shorter as they move toward the back.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the shell
steps.description: Switch to ink. Lay down the shell outline in one committed pass. Add two tiny dots near the front of the shell for the eyes.

steps.title: Ink the claws and arms (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the two claws as chunky teardrops with a small notch at the tip for the pincer. Connect each claw to the shell with two short parallel lines for the arm.

steps.title: Ink the legs (ink)
steps.description: Draw each leg as two short strokes meeting at a slight bend, like a bent knee. Six legs total — three per side. Let the tips end in a point.
